units.enhancementInstructions:
Audience: experienced professionals (45–65). Voice: conversational, executive-friendly, minimal jargon, real-world. Structure the chapter as Concept → Visual → Example → Key Takeaways.
VISUAL: use EXACTLY this brand-coloured Mermaid diagram verbatim — do not redraw, restyle, or rename nodes:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph OLD["Traditional PM"]
      direction TB
      o1["Writes requirements"]:::soft
      o2["Manages the backlog"]:::soft
      o3["Translates needs"]:::soft
    end
    subgraph NEW["AI-Native PM · Context Architect"]
      direction TB
      n1["Designs context"]:::good
      n2["Defines intent & specs"]:::good
      n3["Guides AI"]:::good
      n4["Validates outcomes"]:::good
    end
    OLD ==>|role evolves| NEW

    style OLD fill:#f5f3ff,stroke:#a99ff0,color:#1e1b4b;
    style NEW fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a,color:#14532d;

    classDef anchor fill:#312a8c,stroke:#1e1b4b,color:#ffffff,font-weight:bold;
    classDef good fill:#16a34a,stroke:#0f7a37,color:#ffffff,font-weight:bold;
    classDef soft fill:#ede9fe,stroke:#8b80c9,color:#1e1b4b;
    classDef ghost fill:#ffffff,stroke:#312a8c,color:#1e1b4b;
    classDef warn fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626,color:#7f1d1d;
    classDef gold fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,color:#78350f;
```
Use a real-world enterprise example (Product, Program Mgmt, Architecture, Operations, or AI adoption). Define new terms on first use. Target ≈ 4 page(s). End with a bold “Key Takeaways” list.
